heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

summary.description:
Today, drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 325 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 5184 lux), feels like temperature (up 2.8°C), cloud levels (up 418 m), and outside temperature (up 1.9°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 324.8m lower than yesterday, 418.4m lower than the past week, 246.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 854m to 1496m and averaged 786.8m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

Showers, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and weak sunlight because of clouds. The weather will likely remain the same.
